name: Cascada del Caozo – circular from Valdastillas (PR-CC 32)
name_original: Cascada del Caozo – circular desde Valdastillas (PR-CC 32)
region_intro: Hiking around Valdastillas offers access to the Jerte Valley, a region characterized by steep slopes, terraced cherry orchards, and the Jerte River basin. The landscape features numerous natural waterfalls and provides scenic viewpoints across the valley. The area's terrain includes both gentle paths and more challenging inclines, suitable for various hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around Valdastillas The most popular hiking route is Cascada del Caozo – circular from Valdastillas (PR-CC 32), a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete. This route leads to the impressive Caozo Waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mojón Blanco Trail, a moderate 8.9 miles (14.4 km) path. This trail offers panoramic views of the surrounding Jerte Valley. Local hikers also love the Los Llanos - Calderón Trail, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 48 minutes. Hiking around Valdastillas is defined by its cherry orchards, impressive waterfalls, and scenic viewpoints over the Jerte River. The network of trails offers options for different ability levels, from easy family walks to more demanding routes. The routes in Valdastillas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Valdastillas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Valdastillas?
A: There are over 30 hiking routes around Valdastillas, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 14 easy, 19 moderate, and 2 difficult trails, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking trails in Valdastillas?
A: Yes, Valdastillas offers several easy and family-friendly trails. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990729084/los-llanos-calderon-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Llanos - Calderón Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 5.5 km route that takes about 1 hour 48 minutes. Another excellent choice for families is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17314946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centuries-old chestnut trees – Cherry fields loop from Casas del Castañar&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.5 km long and passes through beautiful cherry orchards.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in Valdastillas?
A: Absolutely! Valdastillas is renowned for its stunning waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990727089/cascada-del-caozo-circular-from-valdastillas-pr-cc-32" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Caozo – circular from Valdastillas (PR-CC 32)&lt;/a&gt; leads directly to the impressive Caozo Waterfall, which is about 30 meters high. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, a popular route featuring six waterfalls and several viewpoints.

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of hikes in Valdastillas?
A: Hiking trails in Valdastillas vary in length and duration. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990729084/los-llanos-calderon-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Llanos - Calderón Trail&lt;/a&gt; are around 5.5 km and take under 2 hours. Moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990739666/mojon-blanco-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojón Blanco Trail&lt;/a&gt;, can be up to 14.4 km and take around 4 hours. There are also longer, more challenging routes for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many trails in Valdastillas are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A popular circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990727089/cascada-del-caozo-circular-from-valdastillas-pr-cc-32" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Caozo – circular from Valdastillas (PR-CC 32)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.2 km long and takes you to the beautiful Caozo Waterfall.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on hikes around Valdastillas?
A: Hikes around Valdastillas offer spectacular views of the Jerte Valley, characterized by steep slopes and extensive cherry orchards. Many trails provide panoramic vistas, such as those found along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5638081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panoramic view of the Jerte Valley&lt;/a&gt; highlight. You can also experience impressive mountain scenery from points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2842817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Honduras Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Valdastillas?
A: The hiking experience in Valdastillas is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning natural landscapes, the impressive waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4360769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caozo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic viewpoints over the Jerte Valley.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or natural pools near the trails?
A: Yes, the Jerte Valley is known for its refreshing waters. You can find natural pools, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5638455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pools of Valdastillas&lt;/a&gt;, which are perfect for a dip during warmer months. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/146290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jerte River&lt;/a&gt; itself also offers opportunities for enjoying the water.

Q: What are some interesting natural attractions to see while hiking?
A: Beyond the waterfalls, you can discover several natural attractions. The area features ancient trees, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5638025" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centuries-old chestnut trees&lt;/a&gt;. The entire Jerte Valley is a highlight in itself, especially during cherry blossom season, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/146290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jerte River&lt;/a&gt; carves out unique landscapes.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Valdastillas?
A: Valdastillas offers beautiful hiking experiences throughout the year. Spring is particularly stunning with the cherry blossoms in the Jerte Valley. Summer provides refreshing waters at the waterfalls and natural pools. Autumn brings vibrant colors to the chestnut groves, and even winter offers unique, tranquil landscapes, though some higher passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2842817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Honduras Pass&lt;/a&gt; might require more preparation.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: While Valdastillas is known for its accessible trails, there are also more challenging options. The guide includes 2 difficult routes for those seeking a greater challenge and elevation gain, offering rewarding experiences for experienced trekkers.
tips: This is undoubtedly one of the most beautiful and impressive waterfalls in the Piornal region. The Caozo waterfall is a wonderful place where you can appreciate nature at its best. It can be reached from Valdastillas following the marked route PR-CC 32.
